factors.summary: Productivity is improving, contributing to higher gross margins and lower costs as a percentage of revenue.

evidence.text: The gross margin was 70 basis points higher than the prior fiscal year’s third quarter gross margin of 43.9%. The increase was primarily driven by lower energy-related expenses. Selling and administrative expenses decreased 5.1% for the three months ended February 28, 2015 , compared to the three months ended February 28, 2014 .
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2015 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2014

factors.summary: Foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations are negatively impacting revenue and increasing selling and administrative expenses.

evidence.text: Uniform Direct Sales operating segment revenue decreased from $337.0 million to $334.9 million, or 0.6%,... Revenue decreased organically by 0.1%. ... First Aid, Safety and Fire Protection Services operating segment revenue increased from $377.2 million to $417.6 million, or 10.7%,... Revenue increased organically by 9.9% as a result of increased sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2015 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 28, 2014

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth shows mixed performance, with a slight decrease in one segment and a significant increase in another. Overall, organic growth is accelerating, driven by increased sales volume across segments.

evidence.text: Organic growth excludes the impact of acquisitions, disposals and deconsolidations, and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations. Revenue growth was negatively impacted 7.2% because of the deconsolidation of the shredding business, which contributed $74.9 million of revenue in the three months ended February 28, 2014, and 0.6% due to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations. Acquisitions positively impacted the growth rate by 0.1%
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2015 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2014
